title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customers are not proof of product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates embedding in customer workflows over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on future AI capabilities rather than current ones.
writeup: Serval CEO Jake Stauch reframes product-market fit as customer pull rather than founder push: real PMF shows up as inbound pricing inquiries, not effort spent dragging deals forward. He cautions that passionate early customers do not confirm fit, and that the signal should be read across deep, embedded customer relationships rather than isolated formal interviews. The talk also makes the case for betting on the trajectory of AI models instead of their current capabilities. The practical takeaways: weigh pull over push, judge PMF from sustained customer understanding, and build for where models are heading.
